title: A zero-sum two-point signal has zero DC mode

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the two entries of a signal sum to zero, then its two-point DC Fourier mode is zero.
assumptions: u0 and u1 are real numbers
the DC mode is u0 + u1

declarations.name: dft2DC
declarations.statement: def dft2DC (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := u0 + u1

declarations.name: dft2DC_zero_sum
declarations.statement: theorem dft2DC_zero_sum (u0 u1 : Real) (h : u0 + u1 = 0) : dft2DC u0 u1 = 0

tasks.input: Translate the zero-DC property of a zero-sum two-point signal into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem dft2DC_zero_sum (u0 u1 : Real) (h : u0 + u1 = 0) : dft2DC u0 u1 = 0

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding dft2DC and using the zero-sum assumption.
tasks.target: Unfold dft2DC and exact the assumption h.
